comment_text.sn38.3:1.2: “Those who teach principles” or “those who teach Dhamma” (dhammavādino).
comment_text.sn38.3:1.4: “Holy One” (sugata) is a frequent epithet of the Buddha, or as here, for any arahant. Formally, it parallels “Realized One” (tathāgata), i.e. “one who has come to the state of truth”. Sugata therefore means “one who has come to the state of the good/happy/well”. These are past participles; the noun form of the same word is sugati, a “good place” or “heaven”. It is a term primarily associated with Buddhism; in later Sanskrit, followers of the Buddha were called bauddha or saugata.

root_text.sn38.3:1.1: “Ke nu kho, āvuso sāriputta, loke dhammavādino, ke loke suppaṭipannā, ke loke sugatā”ti?
root_text.sn38.3:1.2: “Ye kho, āvuso, rāgappahānāya dhammaṁ desenti, dosappahānāya dhammaṁ desenti, mohappahānāya dhammaṁ desenti, te loke dhammavādino.
root_text.sn38.3:1.3: Ye kho, āvuso, rāgassa pahānāya paṭipannā, dosassa pahānāya paṭipannā, mohassa pahānāya paṭipannā, te loke suppaṭipannā.
root_text.sn38.3:1.4: Yesaṁ kho, āvuso, rāgo pahīno ucchinnamūlo tālāvatthukato anabhāvaṅkato āyatiṁ anuppādadhammo, doso pahīno ucchinnamūlo tālāvatthukato anabhāvaṅkato āyatiṁ anuppādadhammo, moho pahīno ucchinnamūlo tālāvatthukato anabhāvaṅkato āyatiṁ anuppādadhammo, te loke sugatā”ti.
root_text.sn38.3:2.1: “Atthi panāvuso, maggo atthi paṭipadā etassa rāgassa dosassa mohassa pahānāyā”ti?
root_text.sn38.3:2.2: “Atthi kho, āvuso, maggo atthi paṭipadā etassa rāgassa dosassa mohassa pahānāyā”ti.
root_text.sn38.3:2.3: “Katamo panāvuso, maggo katamā paṭipadā etassa rāgassa dosassa mohassa pahānāyā”ti?
root_text.sn38.3:2.4: “Ayameva kho, āvuso, ariyo aṭṭhaṅgiko maggo etassa rāgassa dosassa mohassa pahānāya, seyyathidaṁ—
root_text.sn38.3:2.6: Ayaṁ kho, āvuso, maggo ayaṁ paṭipadā etassa rāgassa dosassa mohassa pahānāyā”ti.
root_text.sn38.3:2.7: “Bhaddako, āvuso, maggo bhaddikā paṭipadā, etassa rāgassa dosassa mohassa pahānāya.

translation_text.sn38.3:1.1: “Reverend Sāriputta, who in the world have principled speech? Who in the world practice well? Who are the Holy Ones in the world?”
translation_text.sn38.3:1.2: “Reverend, those who teach principles for giving up greed, hate, and delusion have principled speech in the world.
translation_text.sn38.3:1.3: Those who practice for giving up greed, hate, and delusion are practicing well in the world.
translation_text.sn38.3:1.4: Those who have given up greed, hate, and delusion—so they’re cut off at the root, made like a palm stump, obliterated, and unable to arise in the future—are Holy Ones in the world.”
translation_text.sn38.3:2.1: “But, reverend, is there a path and a practice for giving up that greed, hate, and delusion?”
translation_text.sn38.3:2.4: “It is simply this noble eightfold path, that is:
translation_text.sn38.3:2.5: right view, right thought, right speech, right action, right livelihood, right effort, right mindfulness, and right immersion.
translation_text.sn38.3:2.6: This is the path, this is the practice for giving up that greed, hate, and delusion.”
translation_text.sn38.3:2.7: “This is a fine path, a fine practice, for giving up greed, hate, and delusion.
translation_text.sn38.3:2.8: Just this much is enough for diligence.”
